title: Complete gate constraints: enable relation-hashing and prose-healing
context: Gate-era initiative required hash-driven reconciliation (sqlite never reaches loadAll, reconcile is hash-driven) to satisfy concurrent-safety constraints; this blocked two roadmap items. Touches invariant(s): con_902759b3dc (All JSON writes to .hunch/ must be atomic (temp-file + rename) so an interrupted write can never truncate the index); con_a87360128b (Semantic vectors are a derived layer in SQLite, never the source of truth; reconcile by content hash on reindex).
decision: Verified all four gate constraints satisfied (902 raw writes, better-sqlite3 conformance); implemented relation-hash links (slug-first, pack-hashed, sibling renames cascade); added prose-heal tier (`--prose-heal` for LLM-reconciled adoption with byte-identical degradation).
consequences: Data-flow safety constraints now guaranteed throughout codebase
Sibling-relation changes deterministically re-render all linkers (residual closed)
Adopted documents support LLM-reconciled prose overviews with graceful failure handling
Golden-reference evaluation tests now follow decision-supersession chains
alternatives_rejected: Ship features without completing gate constraints (leaves concurrent-safety gap)
Prose-heal without deterministic fallback (silent failures on LLM issues)

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Ship features without completing gate constraints (leaves concurrent-safety gap)
Prose-heal without deterministic fallback (silent failures on LLM issues)
